findings: Backward packet length features show low cardinality and near-constant behavior in some cases, consistent with class-conditioned structural variation but indicating limited feature variability.
Unique value counts have stabilized, supporting the reliability of the observed variance ratio signals.
Evidence supports the presence of representation-sensitive patterns and structural irregularities in backward packet length features.
limitations: Low cardinality and near-constant signals limit the granularity of variance ratio interpretation.
No direct variance ratio values computed locally, only inferred from cardinality signals.
